nodes.name: Sticky Note

parameters.content:
## Setup
1. Add your **Hubspot** credentials
2. Customize your criterias for assigning deals in the `Assign by Region` and the following `Assign` nodes
3. Make sure deals are assigned to the right salesrep in the Hubspot nodes at the end
4. Activate the workflow

nodes.name: Assign DE deals by company size
Assign US deals by company size

parameters.content:
## Setup
1. Add your **Hubspot** credentials
2. Customize your criterias for assigning deals in the `Assign by Region` and the following `Assign` nodes
3. Make sure deals are assigned to the right salesrep in the Hubspot nodes at the end
4. Activate the workflow
👇 You can adjust the interval here.
Adjust this and make sure the right people are assigned for the criteria
Adjust this and make sure the right people are assigned for the criteria
👈 This is the fallback. You could also notify you on Slack or something instead

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: hubspot  O Auth2  A P I details
configurationSteps: Open the scheduler node "Schedule Trigger".
Adjust the execution intervals (e.g., Every hour, Daily, or Custom CRON expression) according to your preferences.
Open the "Get Deals" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get contact for deals" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Unassigned Deals" node (n8n-nodes-base.filter) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Split out deals" node (n8n-nodes-base.splitOut) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Assign by Region" node (n8n-nodes-base.switch) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Assign deal to John" node (n8n-nodes-base.hubspot) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Assign to Max" node (n8n-nodes-base.hubspot) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Assign to Eeden" node (n8n-nodes-base.hubspot) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Assign to Peter" node (n8n-nodes-base.hubspot) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get company of contact(s)" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Assign US deals by company size" node (n8n-nodes-base.switch) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note3" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note4" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note5" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Assign deal to Edward" node (n8n-nodes-base.hubspot) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Assign to Michael" node (n8n-nodes-base.hubspot) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Assign to Suka" node (n8n-nodes-base.hubspot) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Assign DE deals by company size" node (n8n-nodes-base.switch) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
No global variables node (Set node) is present. If you need dynamic constants, add a Set node after the trigger.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
